title: pongamiopsis pervilleana
intro: Pongamiopsis pervilleana is a plant species in the family Fabaceae, commonly known as the pink hawthorn. This plant is traditionally used in folk medicine, particularly in the Pacific region, for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic compounds. The health benefits of Pongamiopsis pervilleana include its ability to treat various ailments such as fever, skin infections, and respiratory issues. Traditionally, the plant is prepared as a decoction or infusion, which can be used to make herbal teas, ointments, or other preparations for medicinal purposes.
